summary.text: AI agent frameworks and coding agents remain the dominant categories, with revfactory/harness and EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin each gaining hundreds of stars daily, and anthropics/skills continuing its massive sustained run. However, the standout acceleration anomaly is MoneyPrinterTurbo, which added 4,400 stars in 24 hours—a 3x+ jump from its typical velocity—suggesting a surge of interest in AI-driven video production from a single prompt. This cross-source pattern is reinforced by Hacker News top stories focusing on Claude Opus 4.8 and a 'Please Use AI' sentiment, indicating developer curiosity about practical, high-impact AI applications. The TTS category also shows renewed attention with MOSS-TTS's 217-star daily gain, hinting at growing demand for open-source audio generation. If MoneyPrinterTurbo's trajectory continues, it may signal a broader shift from agent frameworks to automated content creation workflows.

projects.name: MOSS-TTS
projects.description: MOSS‑TTS Family is an open‑source speech and sound generation model family from MOSI.AI and the OpenMOSS team. It is designed for high‑fidelity, high‑expressiveness, and complex real‑world scenarios, covering stable long‑form speech, multi‑speaker dialogue, voice/character design, environmental sound effects, and real‑time streaming TTS.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A comprehensive open-source family of speech and sound generation models covering TTS, dialogue, voice design, sound effects, and real-time streaming.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a unified, production-ready suite for high-fidelity, expressive speech synthesis and audio generation in complex real-world scenarios, including long-form speech, multi-speaker dialogue, voice cloning, and environmental sound effects.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 2525 stars and gained 217 stars in the last 24 hours, indicating strong and sustained interest. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), suggesting steady growth rather than a sudden spike. This consistent attention reflects ongoing community engagement and the project's relevance in the TTS space.
llmSummary.whatChanged: Recent activity includes multiple releases in May 2026: MOSS-SoundEffect-v2.0 (DiT-based, 48 kHz, up to 30s), MOSS-TTS-v1.5 (improved multilingual synthesis, voice cloning, and prosody control), and support for mlx-audio. These updates likely drove the recent star increase.
llmSummary.whyTrending: MOSS-TTS offers a rare combination of open-source, high-quality, and diverse audio generation capabilities (TTS, dialogue, voice design, sound effects) with strong performance benchmarks, making it a go-to resource for developers building voice applications.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a versatile toolkit for integrating advanced TTS and audio generation into applications, with support for multiple architectures (Delay, Local, Realtime) and backends (PyTorch, llama.cpp, SGLang). Developers can leverage pre-trained models for zero-shot voice cloning, fine-tuning, and real-time streaming.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift toward open-source, modular TTS systems that rival closed-source solutions. Developers should consider MOSS-TTS for building voice agents, content creation tools, and multilingual applications, especially given its support for on-device inference via llama.cpp.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers, voice application builders, content creators, researchers in speech synthesis, and teams needing production-ready TTS with voice cloning and sound effects.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (first release Feb 2026) and may have limited community support or documentation gaps. Licensing terms should be reviewed for commercial use. The rapid release cadence may lead to instability.

projects.description: A meta-skill that designs domain-specific agent teams, defines specialized agents, and generates the skills they use.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A meta-factory for Claude Code that generates domain-specific agent teams and skills from a single sentence prompt.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Manually designing and configuring multi-agent teams for complex tasks is time-consuming and error-prone. Harness automates the creation of coordinated agent teams with predefined architectural patterns, skill generation, and validation.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 4138 stars and a 24-hour delta of +181, the project shows strong and sustained attention momentum. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This consistent velocity suggests deep developer interest in structured agent team design.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits within 7 days and a version 1.2.0 badge indicate active maintenance, but no major release or external buzz event is detectable.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Harness addresses a critical pain point in the Claude Code ecosystem: moving from single-agent prompts to multi-agent orchestration. Its 'meta-factory' approach (generating harnesses that generate agents) is novel, and the 100 pre-built harnesses in harness-100 provide immediate value. The A/B study showing +60% quality improvement adds credibility.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Harness is a practical tool for anyone building complex workflows on Claude Code. It reduces the overhead of designing agent teams and provides battle-tested patterns. The plugin marketplace installation makes it easy to try.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward meta-orchestration: tools that design other tools. Developers should watch for similar 'factory' patterns emerging across AI agent frameworks, as they enable rapid prototyping of sophisticated multi-agent systems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Claude Code users building multi-step, multi-agent workflows; developers creating domain-specific AI assistants; teams needing structured agent team architectures.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Tightly coupled to Claude Code's plugin system and agent team capabilities. If Claude Code changes its API or agent model, Harness may break. The A/B study is author-measured and pending third-party replication.

projects.name: compound-engineering-plugin
projects.description: Official Compound Engineering plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and more

llmSummary.oneLiner: A plugin suite for AI coding agents that enforces a structured 'plan-review-compound' workflow to reduce technical debt and compound engineering knowledge.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality code and fail to learn from past work, leading to accumulating technical debt. This plugin provides a disciplined workflow (brainstorm, plan, review, compound) that forces agents to plan before coding, review after, and document learnings so each unit of work makes the next easier.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,275 stars and a 24-hour delta of +449, this project is experiencing sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. The project has a recent release and commits within 7 days, showing active maintenance. The combination of high star count, consistent daily growth, and broad platform support (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, and more) makes it a standout in the AI coding agent ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README has been recently updated with expanded installation instructions for multiple platforms (Codex, Copilot, Factory Droid, Qwen Code, OpenCode, Pi, Gemini, Kiro), which may have broadened its appeal. The project also has a recent release (within 30 days) and active commits, suggesting ongoing feature development.
llmSummary.whyTrending: The plugin addresses a critical pain point for developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent output and lack of knowledge reuse. By providing a structured workflow that compounds learning, it promises to make AI-assisted development more reliable and efficient. The broad platform support and clear documentation lower the barrier to adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This plugin is a practical implementation of 'compound engineering' — a methodology that prioritizes planning and review over raw code generation. Developers using AI coding agents should evaluate whether this workflow improves their code quality and reduces rework. The plugin's multi-platform support makes it easy to try across different tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of structured AI coding workflows signals a shift from 'generate and pray' to 'plan, execute, review, and learn.' This plugin is an early example of a pattern that may become standard: AI agents that not only write code but also maintain a knowledge base of past decisions and outcomes. Developers building AI coding tools should consider integrating similar 'compound' mechanisms.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, etc.) who want to improve code quality, reduce technical debt, and systematically capture engineering knowledge.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The plugin enforces a specific workflow that may feel overly ceremonial for small or fast-moving projects. It requires buy-in from the entire team to be effective. The project is tied to the EveryInc ecosystem, which may limit long-term independence. The 80 open issues suggest active development but also potential stability concerns.

projects.name: DeepSpeed
projects.description: DeepSpeed is a deep learning optimization library that makes distributed training and inference easy, efficient, and effective.

llmSummary.oneLiner: DeepSpeed is a deep learning optimization library by Microsoft that makes distributed training and inference of large models efficient and accessible.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Training and inference of large deep learning models (billions to trillions of parameters) is challenging due to memory, compute, and communication bottlenecks. DeepSpeed provides system innovations like ZeRO, 3D parallelism, and MoE to scale training across GPUs while reducing memory footprint and improving throughput.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 42,407 stars and a steady 7-day average star delta of 1.8, DeepSpeed remains a foundational tool for large-scale AI. The recent acceleration (4.8x) and a new release within 30 days indicate renewed interest, likely driven by new features like Muon Optimizer support and SDMA for AMD GPUs. The project's high star count and consistent growth signal enduring relevance.
llmSummary.whatChanged: Recent commits and a new release (within 30 days) introduced Muon Optimizer support and System DMA for ZeRO-3 on AMD GPUs. The README also highlights a best paper award at ASPLOS 2026 and a tutorial, which may have spurred attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: DeepSpeed is the de facto standard for training large models in PyTorch, and its continuous innovation (e.g., ZeRO, MoE, offloading) keeps it at the forefront of AI infrastructure. The recent updates cater to both NVIDIA and AMD users, broadening its appeal.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: DeepSpeed is essential for any developer working with large models. Its optimizations can reduce training costs and enable models that would otherwise be infeasible. The library is mature, well-documented, and integrates with Hugging Face Transformers, PyTorch Lightning, and other frameworks.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, DeepSpeed signals a shift toward system-level optimization as a key differentiator. The ability to train larger models with fewer resources is a competitive advantage. The focus on AMD GPUs and offloading techniques also indicates a move toward heterogeneous hardware utilization.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Deep learning engineers and researchers training large models (1B+ parameters) on multi-GPU clusters, especially those using PyTorch. Also relevant for teams deploying large models for inference with low latency.
llmSummary.riskNotes: DeepSpeed is heavily tied to PyTorch and NVIDIA CUDA, though AMD support is growing. The library's complexity may have a steep learning curve for newcomers. Some features (e.g., MoE) require specific hardware configurations.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A meta-factory for Claude Code that generates domain-specific agent teams and skills from a single sentence prompt.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Manually designing and configuring multi-agent teams for complex tasks is time-consuming and error-prone. Harness automates the creation of coordinated agent teams with predefined architectural patterns, skill generation, and validation.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 4138 stars and a 24-hour delta of +181, the project shows strong and sustained attention momentum. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This consistent velocity suggests deep developer interest in structured agent team design.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits within 7 days and a version 1.2.0 badge indicate active maintenance, but no major release or external buzz event is detectable.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Harness addresses a critical pain point in the Claude Code ecosystem: moving from single-agent prompts to multi-agent orchestration. Its 'meta-factory' approach (generating harnesses that generate agents) is novel, and the 100 pre-built harnesses in harness-100 provide immediate value. The A/B study showing +60% quality improvement adds credibility.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Harness is a practical tool for anyone building complex workflows on Claude Code. It reduces the overhead of designing agent teams and provides battle-tested patterns. The plugin marketplace installation makes it easy to try.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward meta-orchestration: tools that design other tools. Developers should watch for similar 'factory' patterns emerging across AI agent frameworks, as they enable rapid prototyping of sophisticated multi-agent systems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Claude Code users building multi-step, multi-agent workflows; developers creating domain-specific AI assistants; teams needing structured agent team architectures.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Tightly coupled to Claude Code's plugin system and agent team capabilities. If Claude Code changes its API or agent model, Harness may break. The A/B study is author-measured and pending third-party replication.

hotProjects.name: compound-engineering-plugin
hotProjects.description: Official Compound Engineering plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and more

llmSummary.oneLiner: A plugin suite for AI coding agents that enforces a structured 'plan-review-compound' workflow to reduce technical debt and compound engineering knowledge.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality code and fail to learn from past work, leading to accumulating technical debt. This plugin provides a disciplined workflow (brainstorm, plan, review, compound) that forces agents to plan before coding, review after, and document learnings so each unit of work makes the next easier.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,275 stars and a 24-hour delta of +449, this project is experiencing sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. The project has a recent release and commits within 7 days, showing active maintenance. The combination of high star count, consistent daily growth, and broad platform support (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, and more) makes it a standout in the AI coding agent ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README has been recently updated with expanded installation instructions for multiple platforms (Codex, Copilot, Factory Droid, Qwen Code, OpenCode, Pi, Gemini, Kiro), which may have broadened its appeal. The project also has a recent release (within 30 days) and active commits, suggesting ongoing feature development.
llmSummary.whyTrending: The plugin addresses a critical pain point for developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent output and lack of knowledge reuse. By providing a structured workflow that compounds learning, it promises to make AI-assisted development more reliable and efficient. The broad platform support and clear documentation lower the barrier to adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This plugin is a practical implementation of 'compound engineering' — a methodology that prioritizes planning and review over raw code generation. Developers using AI coding agents should evaluate whether this workflow improves their code quality and reduces rework. The plugin's multi-platform support makes it easy to try across different tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of structured AI coding workflows signals a shift from 'generate and pray' to 'plan, execute, review, and learn.' This plugin is an early example of a pattern that may become standard: AI agents that not only write code but also maintain a knowledge base of past decisions and outcomes. Developers building AI coding tools should consider integrating similar 'compound' mechanisms.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, etc.) who want to improve code quality, reduce technical debt, and systematically capture engineering knowledge.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The plugin enforces a specific workflow that may feel overly ceremonial for small or fast-moving projects. It requires buy-in from the entire team to be effective. The project is tied to the EveryInc ecosystem, which may limit long-term independence. The 80 open issues suggest active development but also potential stability concerns.

items.title: 阿里云与Qwen成为UEFA多年全球AI合作伙伴
items.description: 阿里云和Qwen成为UEFA官方独家AI、云计算与电子商务合作伙伴，合作期覆盖2027/2028赛季至2032/2033赛季的UEFA男子俱乐部赛事，以及UEFA EURO 2028。阿里巴巴集团主席蔡崇信表示，将投入云计算、全栈AI及全球电商平台能力，支持赛事运营。合作将利用Qwen大语言模型部署先进AI技术，增强球迷互动与媒体内容体验，并依托阿里云基础设施打造全球沉浸式观赛体验。

llmSummary.riskNotes: Notes are from a single attendee's perspective; may not capture full scope of announcements. High HN engagement reflects interest, not necessarily production readiness.

trendEntities.title: MCP is dead?

llmSummary.whatItIs: A blog post and HN discussion questioning the future of the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open protocol for connecting AI models to external tools and data sources.
llmSummary.whyNow: The post has sparked intense debate (297 upvotes, 288 comments on HN), indicating significant developer interest and uncertainty about MCP's viability, adoption, and standardization.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI/ML engineers, tool builders, platform teams evaluating MCP for agentic workflows, and developers building AI-integrated applications.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: MCP's fate affects how AI models access external tools and data; if it falters, companies may need to adopt alternative protocols or build custom integrations, impacting development costs and interoperability.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Developers should monitor the debate to decide whether to invest in MCP-based integrations or wait for a more stable standard; the discussion highlights practical concerns about complexity, performance, and ecosystem support.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The post is a single opinion piece; high HN engagement reflects interest but not consensus. MCP is still evolving, and its long-term adoption is uncertain. No evidence of widespread production abandonment.

items.title: 阿里云与Qwen成为UEFA多年全球AI合作伙伴
items.summary: 阿里云和Qwen成为UEFA官方独家AI、云计算与电子商务合作伙伴，合作期覆盖2027/2028赛季至2032/2033赛季的UEFA男子俱乐部赛事，以及UEFA EURO 2028。阿里巴巴集团主席蔡崇信表示，将投入云计算、全栈AI及全球电商平台能力，支持赛事运营。合作将利用Qwen大语言模型部署先进AI技术，增强球迷互动与媒体内容体验，并依托阿里云基础设施打造全球沉浸式观赛体验。

items.title: DynoSim：模拟帕累托前沿
items.summary: 现代大语言模型服务难以调优，因为每个部署都涉及模型后端、张量并行形状、预填充/解码分割、Worker数量、调度器设置、路由策略、KV缓存行为、自动扩展阈值和拓扑等相互关联的选择。这些选择在多个层级相互作用，局部优化可能导致瓶颈转移至其他环节。

items.summary: claude-design-card 是一款专为中文内容创作者设计的 Skill。它能将文字、URL 或文章直接转化为可发布的视觉卡片，如公众号首图、小红书图文卡、教程步骤卡等，支持 28 种布局与 10 种主题。其核心价值在于自动化了"写完文章"后最繁琐的流程：自动提炼重点、选择版式、生成 HTML 并截图成 PNG，替代了以往手动使用 Figma 或 Canva 等工具的步骤。该工具开源，适合经常撰写相关内容的创作者尝试。

items.title: LlamaIndex 团队基于 Google Agents API 构建 LlamaParse/LiteParse 智能体模板
items.summary: LlamaIndex 团队基于 Google 新发布的 Agents API 构建了一个模板，使智能体能够访问 LlamaParse 和 LiteParse，从而自动处理非结构化文档。其工作流程为：配置数据与输出的 Git 仓库，将仓库克隆至智能体沙箱，安装 LiteParse CLI 与 LlamaParse SDK 及相关技能，最后通过提示词驱动智能体自主执行任务。该模板最终形成一个可直接使用 LlamaParse 和 LiteParse 处理真实世界文档的智能体。

trendEntities.title: 亲测为实：难以置信的推理速度
normalizedKeys: title:亲测为实 难以置信的推理速度

items.title: 亲测为实：难以置信的推理速度
items.summary: Kog团队在标准数据中心GPU上实现了极高的单用户推理速度，在8× AMD MI300X GPUs上达到3，000 tokens/s，在8× NVIDIA H200上达到2，100 tokens/s。相比常规推理速度（约100-300 tokens/s），实现了10-30倍提升。其核心思路是将LLM解码视为内存流问题，通过协同设计monokernel、重建同步机制、针对性内存访问映射及采用延迟张量并行的Laneformer模型架构，消除了传统流程的阻塞点。

items.title: OpenRouter支持模型生成文件补丁
items.summary: OpenRouter 现已支持 "apply_patch"，这是一个服务器工具，允许任何模型通过 Responses API 使用 V4A diffs 提出文件编辑建议。 模型生成一个补丁（创建、更新或删除文件）。OpenRouter 在服务器端验证 diff 语法。

items.title: 如何使用 NVIDIA MCG Toolkit 实现 AI 模型文档自动化
items.summary: 随着 AI 模型复杂性增加及加州 AB-2013、欧盟 AI 法案等监管框架加强，软件团队面临在模型发布前生成全面、可审计文档的挑战。NVIDIA MCG Toolkit 旨在自动化模型卡创建流程，该文档需涵盖模型工作原理、预期用途、许可证、训练数据与性能等关键信息。

items.title: 亲测为实：难以置信的推理速度
items.summary: Kog团队在标准数据中心GPU上实现了极高的单用户推理速度，在8× AMD MI300X GPUs上达到3，000 tokens/s，在8× NVIDIA H200上达到2，100 tokens/s。相比常规推理速度（约100-300 tokens/s），实现了10-30倍提升。其核心思路是将LLM解码视为内存流问题，通过协同设计monokernel、重建同步机制、针对性内存访问映射及采用延迟张量并行的Laneformer模型架构，消除了传统流程的阻塞点。
